blurb:
If you want a solid night out without driving far, this is the spot. The XD screen is legitimately impressive and the stadium seating means you're not craning your neck around the person in front of you. We've come here for everything from kids' movies to date nights, and it just works every time.

The self-service kiosks make the whole ticketing process quick and painless. Skip the line, grab your seats, and you're in. With over 2,700 reviews sitting at 4.3 stars, it's clearly not just us who keep coming back.

If you're planning a weekend show, get there a few minutes early. Popular showtimes fill up faster than you'd expect.
address: 2401 S Stemmons Fwy, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA

blurb:
If you've got a group with different tastes or just want a lot of food without a long wait, Regal Buffet is worth knowing about. It's a big roomy spot with a wide spread of Chinese dishes and sushi rolls. The variety is the whole point here. You're not coming for an intimate dinner, you're coming because everyone gets to eat exactly what they want.

With over 3,000 reviews at 4.1 stars, the volume of happy people tells you what you need to know. Good for families, good for groups, good for when you're just really hungry and can't decide on one thing.

Pricing is easy on the wallet, which makes it a solid go-to for a casual weeknight out.
address: 251 N Stemmons Fwy, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
Look, sometimes you just want good Tex-Mex at a price that doesn't hurt and Rosa's is that place. We've grabbed breakfast here more than a few times and it punches well above what you'd expect for a fast-casual spot. The housemade tortillas are the reason people keep coming back. Fresh, warm, and you can tell the difference.

Classic Mexican dishes done right without a lot of fuss. Good for a quick lunch, a family dinner, or even breakfast since they open at 6:30am every single day.

It gets busy, especially on weekends, but the line moves fast. Don't let a crowd scare you off.
address: 705 N Denton Tap Rd, Coppell, TX 75019, USA
hours: Monday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 6:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 6:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
This is the place we end up when a group of us can't agree on anything. Big menu, big booths, loud enough that the kids aren't a problem. It's a legit option for a weeknight dinner, a birthday gathering, or just a long Friday with people you actually like.

The house brews are the real draw here. They rotate options and the selection is wider than most places in the area. The pub fare is solid across the board, the kind of food that hits right after a long day without any surprises.

They're open until 1am on Fridays and Saturdays, which is hard to find around here. Good to know if you're looking for a late bite.
address: 2609 S Stemmons Fwy, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 1:00 AM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 1:00 AM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM

restaurant_name: Wu Wei Din Chinese Cuisine - Lewisville
blurb:
This one quietly has some of the best Chinese food in the area and a lot of people still don't know about it. My wife and I have been going back specifically for the scallion pancakes. You can tell they're handmade. Not frozen, not reheated. The twice cooked pork is another one worth ordering, a little sweeter than you might expect but really good.

The Taiwanese three cup chicken also has a following and I get why. If you want something a little different from the usual takeout spots, this is it. Good for a weeknight dinner when you want something that actually feels like it was cooked with some care.

Just know they break between lunch and dinner service, so there's a gap from 3pm to 4pm each day. Plan around that and you're set.
address: 2505 S Stemmons Fwy #3480, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA

blurb:
The Chastain is the place you take someone when you want to actually impress them. It's on Powers Ferry, dinner and brunch service, and the atmosphere delivers before the food even hits the table. The outdoor tent area has a real energy to it, lively even in colder months, and on a nice day it's legitimately one of the better patios in Atlanta.

Brunch is where a lot of people start, and my wife and I did the same. The burger got singled out in pretty much every conversation we heard around us, which is not something that happens with a bad burger. The miso aioli that comes with the fries is worth calling out specifically. The pastries are also in the mix if you want to start sweet. Staff know the menu well and actually engage with you, which matters at this price point.

Monday dinner service isn't offered, so if you're planning a weeknight dinner, Tuesday through Sunday is your window. Worth a reservation either way.
address: 4320 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Atlanta, GA 30342, USA

blurb:
The Select is a dinner spot, full stop. Art Deco design, open kitchen, a conservatory that makes the whole room feel different from anything else in Sandy Springs. It's the place you go when the occasion calls for something that actually feels special. Date night, celebrating something, or just a Friday where you want the full experience and not a quick bite.

The menu is modern European, and the open kitchen setup means you're watching the whole operation while you eat, which adds something to the meal. It's on the expensive side, so set your expectations there, but the atmosphere and the food quality are doing real work to justify it.

They don't open until 4pm any day of the week, so this is strictly an evening operation. Sunday brunch starts at 10am, which is the one exception if you want to try it in the daytime. Either way, a reservation is probably the smart call.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Suite 200, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 4:00 – 11:00 PM, Friday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Saturday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Sunday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
Buttermilk Kitchen is the spot my wife drags me to on Saturday mornings and I never once complain about it. It's a breakfast and lunch place on Roswell Road, locally sourced, made from scratch, and it shows in everything that comes out of the kitchen. The vibe is casual and comfortable. Neighborhood spot energy, not a scene.

The whole menu leans into Southern breakfast done right. This is real scratch cooking, not shortcuts dressed up with a story. It's the place you go when you want a proper weekend brunch without driving too far or fighting downtown traffic.

One thing worth knowing: they're closed on Mondays, and they only run until 2pm on weekdays. Saturday and Sunday they go until 3pm. So this is a morning and afternoon situation only, plan accordingly.
address: 4225 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30342, USA
hours: Monday: Closed, Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM, Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM, Thursday: 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM, Friday: 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM, Saturday: 8:00 AM – 3:00 PM, Sunday: 8:00 AM – 3:00 PM

blurb:
If you've never had Persian food, Yalda is a genuinely great place to start. And if you have, you'll immediately understand why this place has nearly 4,300 reviews and a 4.9 rating. My wife and I went on a weeknight and the room was packed. The interior is beautiful, the kind of space that makes you feel like the meal is going to be an event before you even look at the menu. There's mezzanine seating and covered outdoor seating, and the whole setup has a lot more polish than most spots in a strip around Aria Blvd.

The staff are genuinely helpful here, not in a rehearsed way. They'll walk you through the menu, make real suggestions, and check back on you without being hovering. The food presentation is meticulous. Every plate looks like someone actually cared about how it landed in front of you. The starters are reasonably priced and a smart place to explore. Entrees run a bit higher, but the quality tracks with it.

Just know they break between lunch and dinner service every single day. Lunch wraps up around 2:45pm and dinner doesn't start until 4pm. And you'll want a reservation, especially on weekends. Show up and hope for a table and you're going to be waiting a while.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Ste 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Rio is the move when you've got a group, big appetites, and nobody wants to fuss with a complicated dinner decision. It's an all-you-can-eat Brazilian churrascaria over on Powers Ferry in Marietta, and it does exactly what that format promises. Grilled meats, feijoada stew, and cheese bread that people genuinely talk about. The cheese bread alone is worth the trip for some folks.

This is a relaxed spot. Not fancy, not trying to be. The vibe is casual and comfortable, good for families, groups, or anyone who wants a lot of food without a lot of ceremony. If you've never done Brazilian barbecue, this is a solid place to try it without overthinking it.

They're open seven days a week starting at 10am, so there's flexibility on timing. Easy to fit in on a weekend without planning too far ahead.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd Ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
If you want wings, cold craft beer, and live music on a weekend, this is your place. It's high energy and the outdoor tiki bar is genuinely fun when the weather cooperates. Good for a game day, a night out with friends, or really any time you want a loud, casual spot that doesn't take itself too seriously.

The wings are the main event and they've built a solid reputation around them. Pair them with something from the tap list and you're set. The pub grub rounds out the menu if you want more than just wings.

They're open until 11pm every single day, which makes them one of the better late-night options in this part of Marietta.
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb:
Good spot for lunch with a group, especially if that group includes kids or grandkids who have opinions about where they eat. It's got a casual, laid-back feel and the service has a reputation for being solid. The kind of place that works for a family meetup when you don't want to overthink the decision.

The menu leans toward melts and comfort food. A few reviewers mention the food can run a little greasy depending on what you order, so it's worth keeping that in mind. But most people who say that also say they'd go back, which tells you something.

Open seven days a week, and they stay open until 10pm on Fridays and Saturdays if you're looking for a later dinner option.
address: 800 Whitlock Ave NW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
My wife and I end up here when we want Mexican and don't feel like cooking. It's colorful, lively, and decorated the way a good cantina should be. Works great for a weeknight dinner out or a Friday night when you want something a little more fun than a quick pickup order.

The Mexican staples are what people come for, and the margaritas have a real following. If you're going with a group that wants to sit, eat well, and have a couple drinks, this is a solid call.

They're open until 10:30pm on Fridays and Saturdays, which is later than a lot of places around here. Good to know if dinner runs long.
address: 1860 Sandy Plains Rd, Marietta, GA 30066, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
This one is a hidden gem and I mean that genuinely. It's a taqueria inside a supermarket on Allgood Road, and if you've driven past it without stopping, that's a mistake worth correcting. It's the kind of place where the food is clearly made by people who actually care about what they're cooking.

The pastor is what I keep hearing about most, and the reviews back it up. Well seasoned, cooked right, not overdone. The pupusas also have a serious following, especially the beans and cheese. And the chicken plate with rice and beans? People note the chicken is marinated ahead of time and you can taste the difference. The beans are made from scratch, not out of a can. That matters.

Prices are low and the hours run until close to 10pm every day of the week. Solid for a casual lunch or a quick dinner when you don't want to spend much.
address: 786 Allgood Rd NE, Marietta, GA 30062, USA
hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 9:50 PM, Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 9:50 PM, Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 9:50 PM, Thursday: 9:00 AM – 9:50 PM, Friday: 9:00 AM – 9:50 PM, Saturday: 9:00 AM – 9:50 PM, Sunday: 9:00 AM – 9:50 PM

blurb:
This place has become a regular stop for my wife and me on weekend mornings. It's cheerful, fast, and the kind of spot you walk into once and then start telling everyone about. Good for a casual breakfast, a mid-afternoon coffee break, or grabbing something sweet on the way home.

The Brazilian light bites are worth working through, but the traditional cakes and cookies are what keep people coming back. My wife is partial to the baked goods, and honestly it's hard to leave without grabbing at least one thing from the case.

They're open late every day, which is nice. Most bakeries shut down by late afternoon, but these guys run until 10pm all week. That alone makes them worth bookmarking.
address: 1260 Powers Ferry Rd A, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
